title: amp-140638 [lightning] Tooltip display order
raw_commits:
{"author":"Cursor Agent","message":"fix(core-ui): add z-50 to HoverCardContent to prevent tooltip overlap

The HoverCardContent component was missing a z-index, causing it to render
behind other elements with stacking contexts despite being portaled to
document.body. This was visible on the AI Controls settings page where the
'Roles with create access' hover card was overlapped by adjacent UI elements.

Adding z-50 ensures hover cards always render on top of regular page content,
consistent with how other overlay components in the design system handle
layering.

Co-authored-by: William Newton <williamnewton@users.noreply.github.com>","sha":"28063f869ed877bd5e40d24e3f0a973fcc25e1a0"}
{"author":"Cursor Agent","message":"fix(core-ui): move z-50 from base HoverCardContent to usage site

Per code review feedback, the z-index should not be set on the base
HoverCardContent component. Instead, pass z-50 via className at the
specific usage site in GlobalAgentToggleCards where the tooltip overlap
occurs.

flow. Previously, only ChartInsightArtifact and DashboardAnalysisArtifact

  into notebook rows, including evidence sections with headings

title: AMP-140638 [agents] Extract opportunity detail view to dedicated page route
raw_commits:
{"author":"Claude","message":"amp-140638 [agents] Convert opportunity detail from side drawer to full page

Replace the OpportunityDetail drawer with a dedicated OpportunityDetailPage
at /opportunities/:opportunityId. The detail page includes:
- Breadcrumb navigation back to the opportunities list
- Color-coded metadata pills (status, source, tags) under the title
- Tabs: Overview, Sources (renamed from Relations), Actions, Outcome
- Overview tab with description, source, and details cards
- Sources tab reusing the existing relations component
- Actions and Outcome tabs with empty states
- Not-found state when opportunity ID doesn't match

The opportunities list now navigates to the detail page on click instead
of opening a side drawer.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01A4auicLMWVod4iSEUTSBLz","sha":"3608a97ad6487a7a0fb09be55ef941e8e2e59590"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"Fix embedded entrypoint navigation and remove dead code

- Make Opportunities accept an optional onSelectOpportunity callback prop.
  When provided (e.g. by the embedded entrypoint used in Lightning), it
  calls the callback instead of navigating to the agents-specific detail
  page route. This prevents broken navigation when Opportunities is
  rendered outside the agents app.
- Remove unused statusEntry variable in OpportunityDetailPage.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01A4auicLMWVod4iSEUTSBLz","sha":"bd54024517f8a61120ea0af90e8132f353b5907d"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"Fix StatusPill using hardcoded positive variant for all statuses

Add a variant field to STATUS_REGISTRY so each status maps to a
semantically appropriate badge variant:
- New / Investigating / In Progress → informative (blue)
- Planned / For Review → notice (orange)
- Shipped / Measured → positive (green)
- Dismissed → neutral (gray)

StatusPill now reads entry.variant instead of hardcoding "positive".

https://claude.ai/code/session_01A4auicLMWVod4iSEUTSBLz","sha":"4ca862389c4283411fda60542da5821d053a657f"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"Add skeleton loading state to opportunity detail page

Show a skeleton layout (mimicking the header, pills, tabs, and cards)
while useOpportunities is loading. The not-found state now only renders
after loading completes, preventing a flash of incorrect content during
initial data fetch.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01A4auicLMWVod4iSEUTSBLz","sha":"e0efd16f1a1724df6ea0ce4638f36445d9f8b603"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"Move agents navigation out of Opportunities into OpportunitiesPage

Opportunities is a shared component rendered by both the agents app
(OpportunitiesPage) and the embedded entrypoint (used by Lightning).
Previously it contained agents-specific navigation as a fallback when
no onSelectOpportunity was provided, which broke the embedded case by
navigating to a non-existent agents route.

Now:
- Opportunities has no built-in navigation; it just calls the optional
  onSelectOpportunity callback
- OpportunitiesPage passes the agents-specific navigation handler
- The embedded entrypoint renders without a handler (cards are inert),
  preserving the expected behavior for external consumers
- OpportunityCard.onClick is now optional to support this

https://claude.ai/code/session_01A4auicLMWVod4iSEUTSBLz","sha":"944b0b17a35741f2f048ffbc2411d64d99d492a9"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"Fix opportunity row click navigation and canonicalize tag styling

- Add routing and onSelectOpportunity handler to OpportunitiesEntrypoint
  so clicking a row in the embedded lightning context navigates to the
  detail page
- Derive OpportunityDetailPage back path from the current URL instead
  of hardcoding rewriteToAgentsPath, so it works in both standalone
  agents and embedded lightning contexts
- Expand TAG_BADGE_VARIANT to cover all known tags with semantically
  appropriate badge variants (positive, negative, notice, informative)
  ensuring consistent styling between the list and detail views

https://claude.ai/code/session_01A4auicLMWVod4iSEUTSBLz","sha":"a0d592d798f20ee0b67837aab45fb7d742219847"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"Fix opportunity row click by routing through lightning props instead of router hooks

react-router-dom is not shared via import maps between the agents
microfrontend and lightning, so router hooks inside the dynamically
imported entrypoint cannot access lightning's router context.

Fix by passing routing info as props from lightning into the entrypoint:
- AgentsRoutes: add explicit route for opportunities/:opportunityId

{"author":"Cursor Agent","message":"fix: prevent undefined in experiment/flag URLs when resource ID lacks appId prefix

When an experiment/flag resource ID is a plain ID (e.g., '837199') without
the expected appId prefix format ('appId_resourceId_type'), the
getResourceLocationObject function would incorrectly set:
- appId = the resource ID itself (from parts[0])
- id = undefined (from parts[1] which doesn't exist)

This caused URLs like:
  /experiment/amplitude/837199/config/undefined?source=ai+assist
instead of the correct:
  /experiment/amplitude/{appId}/config/837199?source=ai+assist

Fix: Add parts.length > 1 guard (matching the existing COHORT handler
pattern) for EXPERIMENT, FLAG, RELEASE, ANNOTATION, EVENT, CUSTOM_EVENT,
and property resource types. Also add parts.length > 2 guard for
GROUP_PROPERTY which accesses parts[2].

title: amp-140638 [lightning] FIX BUTTERBAR BROKEN Outage banner positioning
raw_commits:
{"author":"Cursor Agent","message":"fix: center outage butterbar banner by correcting CSS positioning

The .butter-bar-group had left: 50%; right: 50% which created a
zero-width fixed container at the viewport center. The butterbar
content overflowed this zero-width box, causing it to appear off-center
and clipped on the left side of the screen.

Changed to left: 0; right: 0 so the fixed container spans the full
viewport width. The child .butter-bar-container already has
justify-content: center which properly centers the butterbar.
Added pointer-events: none on the parent to avoid blocking clicks
on elements below (the butterbar itself already has pointer-events: all).

Co-authored-by: William Newton <williamnewton@users.noreply.github.com>","sha":"08fab43640a34c083b16befd8ddd4162d10138e4"}
{"author":"Cursor Agent","message":"fix: apply butterbar centering fix to lightning CoreLayout (the actual outage banner)

The outage notification is dispatched through lightning's Redux store,
so it renders in the lightning ButterBarGroup, not the onenav one.
The lightning CoreLayout.module.scss had the same left: 50%; right: 50%
bug creating a zero-width fixed container.

Fixed to left: 0; right: 0 with pointer-events: none to span the full
viewport width. Removed the now-unnecessary sidebar-collapsed variant
and cleaned up unused imports (cx, getIsSidebarCollapsed).

Co-authored-by: William Newton <williamnewton@users.noreply.github.com>","sha":"56af9b7423b46c8b7e77c6dfe8308964e8577d8a"}
{"author":"William Newton","message":"Merge branch 'master' into cursor/outage-banner-positioning-7992","sha":"0f8fe58e45fe32cb7ba95dcb7f0d5bbd858a0310"}
{"author":"Cursor Agent","message":"fix: remove sidebar-collapsed override that broke butterbar centering

The .butter-bar-group uses left: 50%; right: 50% to create a zero-width
anchor at the viewport center. The child .butter-bar-container uses
justify-content: center which causes the butterbar content to overflow
symmetrically around this anchor, appearing perfectly centered.

The &.sidebar-collapsed { left: 0 } override moved the anchor to the
left edge, causing the butterbar to go off-screen to the left. This
rule was a leftover from when the butterbar used left: $sidebar-width
positioning (pre-2023) and was never cleaned up when the centering
pattern was introduced in AMP-76587.

Removed the sidebar-collapsed CSS rule and its corresponding JS
references (cx, getIsSidebarCollapsed, sidebar-collapsed className).

Co-authored-by: William Newton <williamnewton@users.noreply.github.com>","sha":"0b320d486963dc527d52cff8d14a443c1e2a952a"}

title: amp-140638 [lightning] Claude/svg collage background sg7n b
raw_commits:
{"author":"Claude","message":"Add SVG collage background component for AskAISplashPage

Replace static PNG background with a new SvgCollageBackground component
that layers 7 SVGs (bg-blur, 4 ellipses, 2 squiggles) with CSS blend
modes and animations. Ellipses drift organically (25-35s cycles) and
squiggles wobble subtly (8-10s cycles). Includes dark mode adjustments
and prefers-reduced-motion support.

SVG assets to be added separately: bg-blur.svg, ellipse-1–4.svg,
squiggle-left.svg, squiggle-right.svg in the assets/ directory.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NuUdV2vxpuguHxGbauZkBV","sha":"a6f334e353862ff1cbb5c01c1f49da240bbfca4c"}
{"author":"William Newton","message":"add images","sha":"e2bd0f072b19b582aa08b8d27ebf4874b9d82006"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"Refine SVG collage CSS to match actual asset content

- Ellipses 1-2 (blurred gradients): sized to 120-160% width with screen
  blend, bleeds off edges for ambient glow effect
- Ellipses 3-4 (stroke outlines): very low opacity (0.06-0.08), new
  orbit keyframes for slow rotational drift
- Squiggles: sized by height (110%) to fill vertically, no CSS opacity
  override since SVGs already have hard-light blend + 0.1 opacity baked in
- Dark mode: softer blend modes and reduced opacities per layer type

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NuUdV2vxpuguHxGbauZkBV","sha":"ee0beadc22e6ceffc58d8c8108312f08f24d70ad"}
{"author":"williamnewton","message":"auto-fix lint/format issues","sha":"d3b127143825914e2cb28d3f22cfe38f387e240a"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"Fix background z-index overlay and make bg-blur a diagonal color slice

- Add -z-10 to container so background renders behind all page content
- Make bg-blur 250% size with offset positioning so the rotated gradient
  acts as a massive diagonal color wash bleeding off all edges

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NuUdV2vxpuguHxGbauZkBV","sha":"cc9f76c0fb534823464b62c437eef6e607f65e86"}
{"author":"William Newton","message":"Merge branch 'master' into claude/svg-collage-background-sg7nB","sha":"fc55c30388ad9cf588cba0a860c208714e6f28bd"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"Reposition layers toward bottom with white negative space on top

- bg-blur: shifted down (top: 10%) and left (left: -60%) so diagonal
  color band enters from left side ~1/3 down the page
- ellipse-1 (peach): anchored to bottom-right instead of upper-left
- ellipse-2 (lavender): anchored to bottom instead of top
- stroke outlines: shifted up slightly to span white space area
- Background container: z-0, content divs: z-10 for proper stacking

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NuUdV2vxpuguHxGbauZkBV","sha":"2ceb8685a0b40dc764ef718934cceef297db89ab"}
{"author":"williamnewton","message":"auto-fix lint/format issues","sha":"07755282c33621b6d3e737509acf15c4114cc71f"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"Fix layer stacking order to match Figma: lavender behind peach

Figma order (bottom to top):
1. bg-blur (z:0)
2. Ellipse 2 / lavender (z:1)
3. Ellipse 1 / peach (z:2)
4. Ellipse 3 / stroke outline (z:3)
5. Ellipse 4 / stroke outline (z:4)
6. Squiggle left (z:5)
7. Squiggle right (z:6)

Previously ellipse-1 and ellipse-2 were swapped in both DOM order
and z-index. Now lavender sits behind peach, and stroke outlines
render on top of both gradient ellipses.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NuUdV2vxpuguHxGbauZkBV","sha":"c4018935cd14cb1a4fdc7d5cefcb3f6b67a17087"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"Move orange ellipse up, set layers 3-6 to 70% opacity, 2x drift

- Ellipse 1 (peach/orange): moved from bottom:-20% to top:20% so it's
  visible on screen; removed screen blend mode (washed out on white)
- Layers 3-6 (ellipse-1, ellipse-3, ellipse-4, squiggle-left) all set
  to 70% opacity as requested
- Drift animation distances doubled for more pronounced ellipse movement
  (drift1: 25→50px, drift2: 20→40px, etc.)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NuUdV2vxpuguHxGbauZkBV","sha":"61446e05d0a455ea136f2eac8f47bf52ea031e54"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"Anchor gradient layers to bottom with fixed px offsets

Switch from percentage-based positioning to bottom-anchored fixed px
offsets for all gradient/ellipse layers so they stay in the same
vertical position regardless of viewport height:

- bg-blur: bottom: -400px, width: 250vw
- ellipse-2 (lavender): bottom: -200px, width: 160vw
- ellipse-1 (peach): bottom: -150px, width: 120vw
- ellipse-3 (stroke): bottom: 100px, width: 160vw
- ellipse-4 (stroke): bottom: 50px, width: 150vw
- All use vw-based widths so they scale horizontally
- All use height: auto to preserve SVG aspect ratios

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NuUdV2vxpuguHxGbauZkBV","sha":"5d8dee553bec6dd2bedb0e7930adbefb313ed3f6"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"Revert stroke ellipse opacity to subtle values, push bg-blur lower

- ellipse-3: opacity 0.7 → 0.08 (original subtle value)
- ellipse-4: opacity 0.7 → 0.06 (original subtle value)
- bg-blur: bottom -400px → -700px to push gradient further down
  and preserve white negative space at the top of the page

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NuUdV2vxpuguHxGbauZkBV","sha":"cb200ee9befef598585a5014bd300df49e084c84"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"Add dark mode SVG variants for SvgCollageBackground

Gradient layers (bg-blur, ellipse-1, ellipse-2) get dedicated dark SVGs
with deep indigo/purple fills instead of the light pastel palette.
Stroke layers (ellipse-3, ellipse-4, squiggles) use CSS filter: invert(1)
to flip black strokes to white. Removed containerDark opacity hack in
favor of per-layer blend-mode and opacity tuning.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NuUdV2vxpuguHxGbauZkBV","sha":"d347ff8bbc98455112bbfa7347a5e561adbe630d"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"Fix squiggle opacity asymmetry and remove orphaned assets

Add missing opacity: 0.7 to .squiggleRight to match .squiggleLeft,
fixing a lopsided background where the right squiggle was ~43% more
visible. Remove AskAISplashPage.module.css and its 4 PNG backgrounds
(splash-bg-alt-light/dark, splash-bg-light/dark) which had no remaining
imports after the SvgCollageBackground replacement.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NuUdV2vxpuguHxGbauZkBV","sha":"07b2c1a9f7b34778858a4f13b488997b027bdba1"}

{"author":"Claude","message":"refactor(ai-chat): migrate starter prompts from Q&A to action-oriented

Comprehensive overhaul of starter prompts in category-config.ts to shift
from passive question-style prompts to powerful action commands that
leverage MCP tools.

Key changes across all page categories:
- HOME/CHAT: Build dashboards, create funnels, set up cohorts
- CHART: Break down by dimension, add comparisons, create related analysis
- DASHBOARD: Find anomalies, add charts, write shareable summaries
- EXPERIMENT: Analyze results, set up A/B tests, clone experiments
- TAXONOMY: Write descriptions, categorize events, flag duplicates
- SESSION_REPLAY: Find problematic sessions, create cohorts
- COHORT: Compare to all users, create related cohorts, sync destinations
- USER_PROFILE: Map journeys, create similar cohorts, find replays
- And more...

This represents a fundamental shift in how users interact with Amplitude AI -
from asking questions to commanding actions.

Experiments and Feature Flags are different concepts and need different

title: amp-140638 [ai-ui] fix AmpPixelWave clipping by using integer grid and adding padding
raw_commits:
{"author":"Claude","message":"[ai-ui] fix AmpPixelWave clipping by using integer grid and adding padding

The wave animation dots were clipping outside the viewBox due to:
- Using non-integer gridSize (8.53) for position calculations
- Wave Y values could exceed grid bounds
- Line gap caused horizontal overflow

Fixed by:
- Using integer cols/rows (Math.floor of gridSize)
- Clamping wave Y values to valid grid bounds
- Adding centered padding to keep dots within viewBox
- Removing problematic line gap that caused horizontal clipping","sha":"d15112a8b705477a9462c81a9b6acda6c9710b76"}

raw_commits:
{"author":"Claude","message":"Fix chart controls panel collapsing on every edit

The auto-collapse effect was running whenever closeSidebar/openSidebar
callbacks were recreated, which happened on chart control edits when
editId changed. This caused unintended collapses.

Added a ref to track the previous isGlobalAIChatOpen value so we only
collapse/expand when the AI chat state actually changes, not when
callbacks are recreated.","sha":"32afe6b7018b23756728f3dc36ffbbaff2233aaf"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"Initialize chart controls in collapsed state when AI chat is open

Instead of always starting expanded and then animating closed, the chart
controls now check the persisted AI chat state on mount. If AI chat is
persisted as open and the screen is below the auto-collapse threshold,
the controls start in the collapsed state.

This prevents the collapse animation from playing every time when
navigating between links in an open chat thread.

Uses the same 'ai-chat-state' localStorage key that persists the Ask AI
chat window state.","sha":"cc879107183df3aad0ff776fbad0326ad04e278d"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"Export loadPersistedIsOpen from ai-chat-core hooks

The function was being imported from an internal path that isn't
available after build. Now properly exported from the hooks public API.","sha":"2bd73f25e0deb5f7bbb10ae884272dab34e3e06e"}

raw_commits:
{"author":"Claude","message":"CASS-2748 [ai-chat-core] simplify agent activity accordion expand/collapse logic

Change accordion state to derive from isDone instead of isStreaming:
- Expanded when working (isDone=false)
- Collapsed when done (isDone=true)

Use useEffect to sync state when isDone changes (e.g., navigating between
threads) while still allowing users to manually toggle the accordion.

This fixes the issue where navigating between threads would cause in-progress
agent activity accordions to collapse incorrectly.","sha":"ccb1c99b20f1f313c66af20cda0efe0ff7f79538"}

title: amp-140638 [lightning] Auto-collapse Chart Controls on AI Chat
raw_commits:
{"author":"Claude","message":"[lightning] Auto-collapse chart controls when AI chat sidepanel opens

When users click the "Ask AI" button to open the AI chat sidepanel on
chart views, the left chart control container now automatically collapses.
This provides more horizontal space for the chart visualization and
creates a better user experience.

The implementation listens to the global AI chat state from the
@amplitude/ai-chat-core store and triggers the existing collapse
functionality when the chat opens.

Added extensive documentation comments for future extension points,
including conditional collapse based on screen size, user preferences,
chart type, layout mode, and sidebar width.","sha":"852d3f6319065eb72e338008cb2621755fc0ed15"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"[lightning] Auto-expand chart controls when AI chat sidepanel closes

Update the auto-collapse behavior to be bidirectional - controls now
re-expand when the AI chat sidepanel is closed, restoring the original
layout state.","sha":"4558b3f606f2c156a499367f77e7c6e7795a9f5d"}
{"author":"Claude","message":"[lightning] Skip auto-collapse on large screens (> 1880px)

On screens wider than 1880px, there's enough horizontal space for both
the chart controls and AI chat sidepanel, so we skip the auto-collapse
and auto-expand behavior.

Added AI_CHAT_AUTO_COLLAPSE_MAX_WIDTH constant for the breakpoint.
Uses existing windowWidth from useWindowWidth() hook for minimal
performance impact.","sha":"b5e00232216b795fbfaf52e537e9fda99b8acc30"}

title: amp-140638 [ai-chat-core] Hide follow up questions in view only mode
raw_commits:
{"author":"Claude","message":"[ai-chat-core] hide follow-up suggestions in view-only mode

Hide AIChatSuggestions component when viewing shared chat threads in
view-only mode. This ensures users visiting /chat/sessions/* in
view-only mode don't see interactive follow-up suggestions when the

title: amp-140638 [lightning] Fix double scrollbar on /agents/overview on Windows
raw_commits:
{"author":"Claude","message":"[lightning] Fix double scrollbar on /agents/overview on Windows

Remove overflow-auto from AgentsHeader container div to prevent double
scrollbars appearing on Windows browsers. The outer container in
UniversalToolbar already handles scrolling, so the inner overflow-auto
was creating a redundant scroll container.","sha":"0125c25641ac059e8013ca4350548699718d6ce2"}
